resolvedQuestions.question: Is the proposed system-deep-loop target layout structurally sound?
resolvedQuestions.answer: Yes, if runtime remains infrastructure-only and exactly one graph-metadata.json is fresh-authored at the hub.

resolvedQuestions.question: Are the forward and reverse path-coupling repair rules correct?
resolvedQuestions.answer: Yes. Forward runtime-to-workflows paths keep hop count and delete the old segment; reverse workflow-to-runtime paths remove one hop and target runtime/.

resolvedQuestions.question: Does the system-spec-kit tooling-borrow need same-phase repair?
resolvedQuestions.answer: Yes. Runtime typecheck/typeRoots and system-spec-kit council tests point across the old sibling boundary and must move with phase 002.

resolvedQuestions.question: Is the external reference migration plan complete enough?
resolvedQuestions.answer: Mostly yes, with one recommended emphasis: explicitly verify the mk-deep-loop-guard plugin path because it fails open if stale.

resolvedQuestions.question: Should fallback-router.ts be wired now?
resolvedQuestions.answer: Not as a merge blocker. Keep phase 004 optional; run it before a GLM-heavy rerun only if automatic fallback is required.

keyFindings.title: Target layout is coherent if runtime stays infrastructure-only
keyFindings.summary: The proposed layout keeps four workflow packets at hub level and nests former runtime under runtime/ with no mode-registry entry.

keyFindings.title: Forward coupling rule is correct
keyFindings.summary: Runtime-to-workflows imports keep hop count and delete the deep-loop-workflows segment after nesting.

keyFindings.title: Reverse coupling rule is correct
keyFindings.summary: Workflow-to-runtime imports remove one hop and target runtime/ after nesting.

keyFindings.title: Tooling-borrow repair belongs in structural phase 002
keyFindings.summary: runtime and system-spec-kit point at each other for typecheck/test discovery, so this is move-time repair.

keyFindings.title: External reference migration is genuinely broad
keyFindings.summary: Scoped live count found 764 old-name hits across 77 non-spec/non-dist files in external surfaces.

keyFindings.title: mk-deep-loop-guard missing-path behavior is silent
keyFindings.summary: The guard returns null when registry read/parse fails and skips mismatch checks; stale path can silently disable protection.

keyFindings.title: fallback-router.ts is ready but unused by fan-out
keyFindings.summary: The pure router is implemented and tested, while fanout-pool currently settles retry-exhausted lineages without model substitution.

keyFindings.title: Legal convergence reached after 4 iterations
keyFindings.summary: All tracked questions are answered, minIterations is satisfied, and source diversity/quality gates pass.

ruledOutDirections.reason: runtime/ is infrastructure, not invokable mode logic.
Advisor corpus fields, prompt text, executable paths, docs, and historical specs have different safety rules.

ruledOutDirections.approach: Make fallback-router wiring a required merge blocker
ruledOutDirections.reason: It changes fan-out behavior and is explicitly tracked as optional phase 004.
